responsibilities: Receive the lawyer's Telegram, field, Drive and project instructions.
Create or update the main Paperclip matter issue.
Route work to the legal-firm workers without asking the lawyer to choose agents.
Keep final reporting honest about blockers, placeholders, approvals and live Workspace status.
Own the main matter issue, legal strategy and final integration.
Route work to specialist Paperclip workers with clear internal handoffs.
Keep the live file coherent across Telegram, Paperclip, Workspace and local artifacts.
Escalate blockers, approvals and client-facing decisions to the lawyer.
completionGates: Every specialist result is summarized or explicitly marked blocked.
Pending approvals are listed before client-facing delivery.
No matter is closed while specialist blockers remain open.
Paperclip writeback uses MASTER WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Open or reuse clients and matters.
Extract facts from transcripts before asking the lawyer for missing data.
Collect intake in small parts.
Classify missing information by urgency.
completionGates: Client and matter identifiers are known or a blocker is recorded.
Missing fields are classified as para_avanzar, para_firma or no_bloqueante.
Paperclip writeback uses INTAKE WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Assemble matter folders and source indexes.
Maintain the live file as the operational source of truth.
Track source documents, Drive links, Google Docs and local artifacts.
completionGates: All known sources are indexed.
Folder plan has local or Workspace target paths.
Paperclip writeback uses FILE WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Maintain the master control tables for clients, matters, documents, facts and deliverables.
Use local JSON/CSV fallback when live Google Sheets credentials are unavailable.
Prepare dry-run write plans before any live Workspace mutation.
completionGates: Every row has a stable logical key.
Live writes are blocked unless Workspace credentials and approval are present.
Paperclip writeback uses SHEETS WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Identify applicable Mexican legal basis for the requested matter and documents.
Define document requirements and legal risks before drafting.
Separate legal conclusions from missing factual assumptions.
completionGates: Every required document has a legal basis or a recorded research blocker.
Legal risks are classified before drafting starts.
Paperclip writeback uses RESEARCH WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Read sources and transcripts before drafting.
Extract reusable client and matter data.
Apply requested corrections with traceability.
Draft, revise and package legal documents without reducing substance into summaries.
completionGates: No final/signature-ready claim is allowed while placeholders remain.
Facts used in documents are source-backed or marked unknown.
Cross-document review is required for packages.
Paperclip writeback uses LEGAL WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Review privacy, ARCO, sensitive data and compliance obligations.
Map data subjects, controllers, processors and transfer flows.
Block delivery when privacy-sensitive facts are missing.
completionGates: Privacy notices identify data categories, purposes, transfers and ARCO mechanism.
Sensitive data handling is explicit or blocked.
Paperclip writeback uses PRIVACY WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Review software development, licensing, confidentiality and IP ownership issues.
Track deliverables, repositories, third-party components and cotitularity terms.
Block signature-ready status when ownership or scope is ambiguous.
completionGates: Software scope and IP ownership are consistent across NDA, development contract and cotitularity agreement.
Third-party component risks are marked known, unknown or not applicable.
Paperclip writeback uses IP SOFTWARE WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Prepare litigation strategy, procedural posture, pleadings and evidence tables.
Identify judicial deadlines and evidentiary blockers.
Coordinate with Plazos before any filing recommendation.
completionGates: Deadline handoff is created for every identified judicial term.
Evidence gaps are classified before filing.
Paperclip writeback uses LITIGATION WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Register judicial and operational deadlines.
Maintain reminders and escalation status.
Report upcoming deadlines to Despacho Legal.
completionGates: Every deadline has owner, due date, source and escalation rule.
Blocked or missing dates are explicitly reported.
Paperclip writeback uses DEADLINE WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Track retainers, partial payments, balances and final payments.
Coordinate payment-related approvals.
Report collection blockers before matter close.
completionGates: Money movement is never invented and must cite source or approval.
Matter close is blocked when required payment data is missing.
Paperclip writeback uses BILLING WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Convert approved legal drafts into client-facing Kami/Canva-style deliverables.
Preserve legal length, clause structure and defined terms.
Run visual and text QA before delivery links are reported.
completionGates: Editorial output cannot shorten or remove legal substance.
Rendered files pass text and visual QA.
Paperclip writeback uses EDITORIAL WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Review legal work before client delivery or signature.
Identify legal, factual, drafting and delivery risks.
Decide whether the package is blocked, review, client-deliverable or signature-ready.
completionGates: Signature-ready requires zero blockers and zero placeholders.
Client delivery requires an explicit delivery decision.
Paperclip writeback uses SENIOR REVIEW WORK PRODUCT.

responsibilities: Maintain template library and knowledge base proposals.
Prepare weekly and matter-level reports.
Capture lessons learned after completed work.
completionGates: Template changes are proposals until approved.
Reports separate facts, blockers and recommendations.
Paperclip writeback uses KNOWLEDGE WORK PRODUCT.
